plan.description: This project aims to experimentally quantify the movement ecology of Posidonia oceanica fruits under controlled conditions, using a current flume to simulate realistic combinations of currents and wind observed at sea. We aim to measure fruit speeds under 10 current and 8 wind intensities and 2 wind directions to provide useful data to model dispersal distances and trajectories of fruits at sea. In addition, improved identification of beach-cast sites could facilitate seed collection efforts, making seed-based restoration techniques more practical and scalable. This approach would support the production of seedlings for transplantation, facilitating both aquaculture applications and large-scale Posidonia oceanica restoration initiatives.
plan.title: PID: 35883 – Fabio Badalamenti Posidonia oceanica fruit at Kristineberg Center, University of Gothenburg-Wind or Current: Determining the key driver inseagrass floating fruit dispersal to optimize seed collection foraquaculture/restoration purposes

blueprint.label: AQUASERV DMP Blueprint v1

systemField.label: Title of Plan
systemField.description: Please provide a name for your DMP Plan

systemField.label: Description
systemField.description: Briefly describe the context and purpose of the DMP

systemField.label: Language
systemField.description: Select the language of your DMP

referenceField.label: Researchers
referenceField.description: Add here the names of people that have produced, processed, analysed the data described in the DMP.

referenceField.label: Organizations
referenceField.description: Add here the names of the organizations contributing to the creation and revision of the DMPs

extraField.label: Introduction
extraField.description: Ethical considerations in science become crucial whenever your research involves potential harm to living organisms, ecosystems, or communities. If your research fits into these criteria please answer following questions. As a default please fill the Horizon Europe - Ethics template, however other templates are available.

extraField.label: Introduction
extraField.description: Please select a template to describe your dataset that best fits your research topic. You can chose more than one template. All the templates prepared for the AQUASERV project are called "AQUASERV | category | datatype". If you can't find a template that fits your research, please contact aquaservhelpdesk@ualg.pt

blueprint.description: This is the main AQUASERV blueprint containing common questions about you and your project, as well as multiple "templates." The common questions need to be answered only once. Use one "template" for each dataset you are generating. For example, if you generate genomics data, use the genomics template (already available); if you generate LC-MS data, use the LC-MS template (coming soon). If you generate more than one type of dataset, you can combine multiple templates into one DMP "plan."

blueprintValue.value:
Posidonia ocenica is a species endemic to the Mediterranean Sea and therefore alien to Swedish waters. However, our experiment will be carried out indoors, and the protocols to prevent Posidonia oceanica from becoming invasive will be implemented.
All necessary protocols will be strictly followed to prevent it from becoming invasive (although this risk is very low since P .oceanica needs warmer water to thrive). All fruits used in the experiment will be returned to Italy at the end of the experiment. In addition, the flume, the aquariums and equipments used will be thoroughly sterilised after the experiment, and the water will be filtered and sterilised using UV light. This procedure has already been successfully carried out at the Seagrass Ecology Laboratory at the Kristineberg Marine Station, under the supervision of Dr Eduardo Infantes, where all the necessary equipment is available on site.
Dataset will consist of morphological measures of fruits and on a dispersal model to be constructed and developped ad hoc. All data will be made available though reports and scientific papers

reference.label: Institute for the Study of Anthropic Impact and Sustainability in the Marine Environment

description.description: The EU Restoration Law and the Habitats Directive are key frameworks relevant to this proposal. The EU Restoration Law, part of the European Green Deal, emphasizes the restoration of ecosystems critical to biodiversity and carbon sequestration, such as P. oceanica meadows. These meadows are recognized as essential habitats under Annex I of the Habitats Directive (92/43/EEC), which mandates member states to maintain or restore their natural habitats to a favourable conservation status. Understanding fruit dispersal pathways and potential settlement areas will enhance the success of restoration efforts and inform strategies for compliance with EU biodiversity goals. Furthermore, the outcomes of this study will contribute to the EU Mission "Restore our Ocean and Waters by 2030" by supporting evidence-based restoration techniques for degraded seagrass meadows, thus addressing both ecological and policy-driven restoration priorities.
description.label: Horizon Europe - Ethics

descriptionTemplate.label: Horizon Europe - Ethics
descriptionTemplate.description: A template of Horizon Europe about ethics.

fieldSet.title: Are there, or could there be, any ethics or legal issues that can have an impact on data sharing? These can also be discussed in the context of the ethics review. If relevant, include references to ethics deliverables and ethics chapter in the Description of the Action (DoA).
Will informed consent for data sharing and long term preservation be included in questionnaires dealing with personal data?
